content.full_text:
June 11, 1990

Sandy Taylor  
Fair Political Practice Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This letter is written at the direction of Linda Bunch of the Registrar of Voters office in San Diego County with reference to a conversation she had with you on June 7, 1990. She has referred me to you to obtain a written judgement with reference to purchasing a "stamp" which would be used on all outgoing mail; the ability for our contract promoters to utilize the stamp on outgoing mail; and, the ability for our contract promoters to end the solicitation with a comment stating the association is endorsing a certain candidate.

I do not think you have any questions regarding the stamps but I would like to clarify the use of our promoters.

We currently have under contract (2) promotional companies, one company is soliciting the public for our annual rodeo and the other company is soliciting for a drug manual. Both solicitations in length take less than (1) minute and we would like the ability to close the conversation with "and don't forget to vote in November for the DSA candidate for Sheriff, Jack Drown." Neither company will be compensated additionally for this comment.

I understand from Linda you have given the go ahead, so to speak, but we would appreciate your stamp of approval should any questions arise out of this method of endorsement.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Roxanne Heacock, Office Manager  
S.D.C.D.S.A.

cc: James Blackmon, PAC Chairman
